title: Syrian-Style Paella with Pork Belly
description: A fusion dish that combines the flavors of Syria and Spain. The creamy and fermented notes of the pork belly pair perfectly with the savory paella.

ingredients.name: Pork belly
ingredients.notes: Score the fat in a crisscross pattern

ingredients.name: Paella rice
ingredients.notes: Short-grain rice is best for paella

ingredients.name: Fermented yogurt
ingredients.notes: Replace with plain yogurt if not fermented

ingredients.name: Syrian spice blend
ingredients.notes: A mix of cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and allspice

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Adds a smoky depth to the dish

ingredients.name: Chicken broth
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium broth for best flavor

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the pork belly and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the Syrian spice blend, smoked paprika, and chicken broth. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the paella rice and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the fermented yogurt and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
